summary: Package exfiltrates data to author-controlled Telegram/Firebase and ships hardcoded credentials
details: Package '0e' installs a process-level uncaughtException handler at module load that POSTs error stack traces to a hardcoded Telegram bot/chat. It additionally exposes a global Proxy 'L' whose methods exfiltrate arbitrary caller data to multiple hardcoded Telegram chat_ids and to https://iiilll.firebaseio.com. It ships a hardcoded Telegram bot token and an imgbb API key redistributed to every installer, corroborated by a static rule a static pattern match). Three independent block signals: (1) unconsented data exfiltration to attacker/author-controlled infrastructure (Telegram chat platform pattern), (2) hardcoded provider credentials distributed publicly (matches 'pypi-hardcoded-api-keys' generalized to npm), (3) global namespace pollution that silently triggers network I/O. Whether the author intended malice or 'personal logging helper' negligence is immaterial — users have not consented to having exceptions/logs shipped to a third party.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
